channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Swati Maliwal Alleges Assault by AAP Aide and Joins BJP, Criticises Party Leadership
item.description: Swati Maliwal, Rajya Sabha MP, has publicly recalled the alleged assault she faced in 2024 while associated with the Aam Aadmi Party (AAP). She stated that after supporting Arvind Kejriwal for many ye...

item.title: COA affirms disallowance of P195-million payment to PPA for COVID-19 services
item.description: The Commission on Audit (COA) has upheld its 2023 disallowance against the Philippine Ports Authority (PPA) for a P195-million payment made for health and laboratory services during the COVID-19 pande...

item.title: Brandon Smith leaves the field with injury in NRL match
item.description: Brandon Smith, the Melbourne Storm hooker, was forced to leave the field in their latest NRL match after sustaining an injury. The player, who was seen heavily strapped up, was heading towards the tun...

item.title: Daughter Honours Her Father with Car Gift and Emotional Tribute
item.description: In a heartwarming display of love and gratitude, a young woman has captured the attention of many online after giving her father a beautiful car. The tribute came after a touching moment where the dau...

item.title: K Kavitha launches Telangana Jagruthi to pursue regional aspirations
item.description: K Kavitha, daughter of former Telangana Chief Minister K Chandrasekhar Rao, formally launched her new political party, Telangana Jagruthi, after leaving the Bharat Rashtra Samithi (BRS) earlier this y...

item.title: Crowds gather at WA State War Memorial to honour Anzacs
item.description: On Anzac Day 2026, tens of thousands of Australians gathered at the Eternal Flame at the Western Australia State War Memorial to pay tribute to the Anzacs who fought to defend the nation's values and ...

item.title: Iranian Foreign Minister meets Pakistan’s military leadership as Islamabad pushes to restart US-Iran dialogue
item.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi visited Islamabad and held a key meeting with Pakistan’s Chief of Army Staff and Chief of Defence Forces, Field Marshal Asim Munir, as diplomatic efforts intens...

item.title: Urbanisation and the Rise of Durban’s Vervet Monkey Population: Scientific Insights
item.description: Urban vervet monkey populations in north Durban have surged to levels 6 to 12 times higher than the natural ecological capacity of the region’s coastal forests, according to recent research by Pete Gr...

item.title: What You Need to Know About the Inheritance Tax Changes for UK Pensions in 2027
item.description: Starting in April 2027, pensions in the UK will no longer be exempt from inheritance tax (IHT). For many, this means their pension savings could be taxed as part of their estate if they pass above the...

item.title: Drama erupts during I'm A Celebrity final as David Haye confronts Adam Thomas
item.description: The live final of this year's I'm A Celebrity... South Africa was marked by intense exchanges and heated confrontations. Boxer David Haye launched a forceful rant at actor Adam Thomas, accusing him of...

item.title: California cities move to legally recognize polyamorous families amid legal challenges
item.description: Several California cities, including West Hollywood, are taking steps to formally recognize and protect multi-partner relationships, aiming to address housing and healthcare inequities faced by polyam...

item.title: US and Iran remain at odds as indirect talks continue in Islamabad amid ongoing conflict
item.description: US negotiators are set to travel to Islamabad for renewed diplomatic efforts, but Iran has made it clear it will not engage in direct talks with American officials. Instead, Tehran plans to communicat...

item.title: UST Tigresses clinch Final Four berth after eliminating FEU Lady Tams
item.description: In UAAP Season 88 women’s volleyball action, the UST Golden Tigresses secured their place in the postseason after defeating the FEU Lady Tamaraws in a crucial knockout match. The victory formally elim...

item.title: Limited investigative capacity weakens action against BEE fronting in South Africa
item.description: The B-BBEE Commission has acknowledged that companies engaging in fronting are managing to avoid accountability, largely due to severe capacity constraints within the watchdog. According to its latest...

item.title: Canadian Trans Activist Files Human Rights Complaints Over Media Coverage
item.description: Jessica Simpson, a Canadian trans activist previously identified as Jessica Yaniv, has filed multiple complaints with the British Columbia Human Rights Tribunal against media outlets and commentators,...

item.title: Reevaluating the Role of the Governor General: A Call for a New Kind of Leadership
item.description: The article reflects on the role of the Governor General of Canada, urging for a change in how this office is approached. It emphasizes that the position should be filled by someone who represents the...

item.title: Kerala to experience summer rains starting April 27, yellow alert for four districts
item.description: The Kerala state is expected to receive summer rains starting April 27, 2026, as per the latest forecast by the India Meteorological Department (IMD). The rains are likely to intensify over the next f...

item.title: Iran executes accused Mossad spy amid widening wartime crackdown
item.description: Iran executed a man it said had worked with Israel’s Mossad intelligence service, according to state-linked reporting. Authorities described him as part of a wider crackdown that has intensified amid ...

item.title: Protests over electricity issues cause disruption at Diepkloof Hostel in Soweto
item.description: Johannesburg metro police are closely monitoring a tense situation at the Diepkloof Hostel in Soweto after local residents staged protests over ongoing electricity supply problems. Protesters blocked ...

item.title: Key Global and Local Events Captured in Photos, April 18-24, 2026
item.description: From political events to cultural milestones, the week of April 18-24, 2026 was marked by significant developments around the world. In the Philippines, Vice President Sara Duterte faced intense scrut...
